last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: The current ambient goal looks like a pure arithmetic/list-size residual with no visible definitions or route-changing frontier.
navigation.why_now: current goal is a pure residual; try a small closing probe before switching proof family.

preconditions: Use only for pure residual arithmetic/list-size goals.
Probe first; if SMT fails, inspect lemma_hints rather than adding random lemmas.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: The residual closes, or the failure reveals a missing arithmetic or local size lemma.

anti_routes.reason: No program frontier is visible; structural program tactics are the wrong proof family.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect lemma_hints or lookup_symbol for the missing arithmetic/list lemma
downgrade this route and reread current_goal for a hidden definition

moves.title: Read-only tactic probe
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
evidence: ambient goal has a leading implication before the proof judgment
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

evidence: Ambient front-end found a pure residual goal; simplify before asking SMT to solve the remaining logic.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

cut_position.tactic: seq 13 : (size G3.cilog <= PKE_.qD /\ G1.w <> zero /\ G1.g_ = g ^ G1.w) 1%r ((PKE_.qD%r / order%r) ^ 3 * (PKE_.qD%r / (order - 1)%r)) 0%r 1%r.

obligation_families.effect: The current proof point is dominated by local logical obligations rather than program-frontier movement.
obligation_families.limitations: Quantifier names are not normalized across EasyCrypt pretty-printer variants.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

current proof state has no program-frontier work before the logical residual

proof_piece_memory.interpretation: This is manager-owned evidence about prior route repairs and daemon-verified route options. Replayable pieces should still be verifier-checked before commit; stale and negative pieces are warnings, not tactics.

rewrite_anchor.current_tactic: seq 13 : (size G3.cilog <= PKE_.qD /\ G1.w <> zero /\ G1.g_ = g ^ G1.w) 1%r ((PKE_.qD%r / order%r) ^ 3 * (PKE_.qD%r / (order - 1)%r)) 0%r 1%r.

lineage_briefing.interpretation: Manager-owned continuity notes for this node. Use them as resume/navigation evidence; EasyCrypt replay still decides whether any old route piece is valid.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Need local or standard-library lemma names before rewriting a pure goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and qualified-name lookup guidance

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid EasyCrypt form for rewrite, apply, conseq, while, or SMT setup.
